content.text:
Module:
Administration of Systems and Networks  |
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEM3
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Train students in the profession of systems and network administrator with extensive practical workshops (Linux and Windows platforms)
Install, configure and study in practice the main services common in companies equipped with a computer network
Handle best practices for managing, installing and configuring a workstation fleet in a heterogeneous environment
Recommended Prerequisites
Networks, operating systems
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Client/Server Administration:
Client/Server architecture structure
Client/Server models: P2P, two-tier, three-tier
Interaction models: message communication, event communication, database (ODBC), RPC client/server
Object-based client/server
System interoperability, redundancy, and load balancing
Unix Network Administration:
Unix implementation of application service administration
Unix exploitation philosophy, RedHat
Enterprise network configuration (Network Manager, Interface Teaming, VLANs, SSH Tunneling)
Practical user account and permission management
Setting up common enterprise services (Apache, NGINX, DNS, DHCP, etc.)
Latest trends: KVM virtualization, Docker containerization and native containers
Windows Network Administration:
Active Directory domain, distributed resource access architecture, high availability
Enterprise network services setup, Remote Desktop Services, remote access
Internet application, server and workstation deployment
Architecture security and attack surface reduction
Windows network infrastructure lifecycle
Assessment
Exams: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
G. Gardarin, O. Gardarin, "le client serveur", Eyrolles, 1996.
Robert Orfali, Dan Harkey, Jeri Edwards, "Client/Serveur", Vuibert informatique, 1999.
T. Deman, G. Desfarges, F. Elmaleh, M. Van Jones, Window server 2016, Eyrolles, 2017.
T. Bartolone, Red Hat Enterprise Linux CentOS: Mise en production et administration de serveurs, 2019.
